Prism Analytics

Staff Data Engineer

LOCATION

San Francisco, CA (Hybrid)

TYPE

Full-time

COMPENSATION

$220,000 - $300,000 per year

TECH STACK

Apache Spark

Apache Airflow

dbt (data build tool)

Snowflake

Python

SQL

Apache Kafka

Scala

Docker & Kubernetes

Git & CI/CD

About Prism Analytics: Prism Analytics is a fast-growing data
infrastructure company that helps enterprises unlock the value of their
data through modern, scalable analytics platforms. Founded in 2018, we
serve Fortune 500 companies across finance, healthcare, and e-
commerce, enabling data-driven decision-making at scale. Our platform
processes petabytes of data daily and serves thousands of analysts and
data scientists globally.

We are seeking an exceptionally talented Staff Data Engineer with 8-
12+ years of experience and a proven track record of technical
leadership to join our Data Infrastructure team. In this role, you will
architect and lead the design of large-scale data systems supporting
thousands of concurrent users, mentor a team of 4-6 engineers, and set
technical standards across our data platform. You'll work on mission-
critical systems including our real-time streaming pipelines, data
warehouse optimization, and orchestration frameworks. This is an
opportunity to influence data engineering practices organization-wide
and directly impact how enterprises make data-driven decisions.

RESPONSIBILITIES

Design, architect, and lead the implementation of enterprise-scale
data systems processing 50+ TB daily, making critical technical
decisions that impact the entire data infrastructure organization

Mentor and provide technical guidance to 4-6 data engineers,
conducting code reviews, establishing best practices, and fostering a
culture of technical excellence and innovation

Own the technical roadmap for the data orchestration layer (Airflow),
leading cross-functional initiatives with analytics, platform, and
infrastructure teams to improve reliability, performance, and
developer experience

Architect and optimize our Snowflake data warehouse strategy,
including schema design, partitioning strategies, and cost
optimization initiatives resulting in 30%+ efficiency gains

Lead the design and implementation of real-time streaming solutions
using Apache Kafka and Spark Structured Streaming, handling high-
velocity data ingestion with sub-second latency requirements

Establish data quality frameworks and dbt best practices across the
organization, including promotion of modular transformations,
testing strategies, and documentation standards

Transform complex data into actionableinsights for enterprise intelligence Conduct technical interviews and participate in hiring for the data
engineering team, helping identify and attract top talent aligned with
our technical vision

Drive initiatives to improve system reliability, scalability, and
observability; own SLAs for critical data pipelines and drive post-
incident analysis and prevention

Represent the data engineering team in architectural discussions,
providing thought leadership on emerging technologies and

recommending adoption of new tools (e.g., advanced SQL
optimization, data governance)

REQUIREMENTS

8-12+ years of professional data engineering experience with
demonstrated progression to senior/lead roles involving significant
technical responsibility and cross-team collaboration

Expert-level proficiency in SQL and Python, with ability to optimize
complex queries and write clean, maintainable production code

Deep hands-on experience building and maintaining large-scale data
pipelines using Apache Spark, Apache Airflow, and modern data
warehousing solutions (Snowflake, BigQuery, or Redshift)

Proven experience with real-time data systems including Kafka,
message queues, and stream processing frameworks

Strong background in data modeling, dimensional design, and
optimization of analytical databases; experience with dbt or similar
transformation frameworks

Demonstrated technical leadership: mentoring engineers, setting
architectural standards, and driving adoption of best practices across
teams

Experience working on distributed systems at scale; strong
understanding of data consistency, fault tolerance, and system design
trade-offs

BONUS POINTS

Experience with data governance, metadata management, and data
lineage tools

Background in cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and
infrastructure-as-code practices

Track record of leading technical projects from conception through
production deployment

Knowledge of advanced analytics and machine learning data
pipelines

 Contributions to open-source data engineering projects (Airflow, dbt,
Spark, etc.)

BENEFITS

Highly competitive compensation package with generous equity
grants and bonus structure (15-20% annual bonus)

Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance with 100%
premium coverage for employees

Unlimited PTO policy with a minimum of 4 weeks encouraged
annually, plus 10 company holidays

Professional development budget of $5,000 per year for conferences,
courses, and certifications

Remote-first culture with option for 2-3 days per week in office for
collaboration and team building

Parental leave policy: 16 weeks for primary caregivers, 10 weeks for
secondary caregivers

Mental health and wellness programs including subsidized gym
membership and meditation app access

Commuter benefits and annual laptop upgrade for all engineers

APPLY

To apply, please submit your resume, a cover letter highlighting your
experience with large-scale data systems and technical leadership, and a

portfolio or GitHub link demonstrating your work. Apply directly
through our careers page at prism-analytics.com/careers or email
careers@prism-analytics.com with the subject line 'Staff Data Engineer
Application'. We review applications on a rolling basis.
